reference: I read your question carefully, and I understand your concern. Regarding your first question, yes, it is normal to wait for a week or so; it doesn't significantly affect the tumor's progression while careful planning can improve the outcome. As for your second question, it is a difficult one to answer. There are many types of brain tumors, some more aggressive and some less. Each type carries a different prognosis, which will be better determined after the surgery and the biopsy results. I hope everything works out for the best.
text: I read your question carefully, and I understand what you're saying. A very nervous question. Yes, it isn't always the way you want to proceed. It takes some significant imperfection to make a progression up to where you're going and where you think you need to come back from. As for your second question, it is a difficult one to answer. There are many types of interviews. Some are more aggressive and selfless. Each type has a different process. You need to have a different approach. You need to have experience. I don't have any words to answer your question.
